items.headline: Spain punch the first ticket to Sunday's final — 2-0 over France
items.body: La Roja shut down Mbappé and France in Dallas. Oyarzabal buried a first-half penalty after Yamal drew the foul, and Pedro Porro slotted the second. Spain now waits on tonight's winner.
items.say: Did you catch Spain? They completely smothered Mbappé — they look scary good heading into the final.

items.headline: Messi vs. England — for the very first time — with a final spot on the line tonight
items.body: The other semifinal is this afternoon in Atlanta, and it's a doozy: in 21 years for Argentina, Messi has somehow never played England. Winner takes on Spain on Sunday.
items.say: Argentina-England is on this afternoon — wild that Messi's never actually faced England before. You watching?

links.text: Al Jazeera · Preview & how to watch

items.headline: Cooper Flagg's pro debut — the No. 1 pick edges Bronny in Vegas
items.body: The Mavs' rookie phenom had a rocky shooting night (10 pts on 5-of-21) but stuffed the stat sheet and made the winning plays, beating Bronny James's Lakers 87-85 in front of a packed house.
items.say: Cooper Flagg made his Summer League debut against Bronny — messy shooting, but you can see why everyone's hyped.

items.headline: AL blanks the NL 4-0 in Philly — Bellinger takes home MVP
items.body: Eleven AL pitchers combined for a three-hit shutout — the first All-Star shutout since 2013 — and the Yankees' Cody Bellinger drove in the game's only runs with a two-run single in the first.
items.say: The All-Star Game was a pitching clinic — a shutout, and Bellinger grabbed MVP. First one since 2013.

items.headline: Last to first: Wærenskjold wins the fastest road stage in Tour history
items.body: A day after finishing dead last at Le Lioran, Søren Wærenskjold slingshotted through the bunch to win Stage 11 in Nevers at a blistering 50.9 kph — a new record. Pogačar keeps the yellow jersey.
items.say: This cyclist went from dead last one day to winning the fastest stage in Tour history the next — it's a great story.

items.headline: Gracie Abrams drops 'Daughter From Hell' this Friday
items.body: The pop breakout's third album lands July 17 — 16 tracks made with Aaron Dessner, teed up by singles 'Hit the Wall' and the road-trip-in-a-hot-air-balloon video for 'Look at My Life.'
items.say: Gracie Abrams has a new album Friday — that 'Look at My Life' video with the hot air balloon is a whole vibe.

items.headline: Will Ferrell chases one last major in Netflix's 'The Hawk'
items.body: Ferrell's first TV comedy drops Thursday: he plays washed-up golf pro Lonnie 'The Hawk' Hawkins, dragging his ex (Molly Shannon), caddie (Fortune Feimster) and rival son (Jimmy Tatro) into the chaos.
items.say: Will Ferrell's playing a washed-up golf pro in a new Netflix show — the trailer alone had me.

items.headline: Tomorrowland lights up Belgium this weekend
items.body: The world's biggest EDM festival is back in Boom, Belgium starting Friday — David Guetta, Martin Garrix and Calvin Harris headline. Can't make the trip? The livestream is basically its own event.
items.say: Tomorrowland kicks off this weekend — even the livestream looks unreal. Have you ever wanted to go?

items.headline: 'The Ultimatum: Marry or Move On' is back for Season 4 today
items.body: Nick and Vanessa Lachey return as six couples who can't agree on marriage swap partners for the ultimate reality-TV pressure cooker. New episodes hit Netflix today.
items.say: New season of The Ultimatum is out — it's trashy in the best way. Perfect summer background TV.
